creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text:
In ports/ there's convention to have unsuffixed package track the latest stable or be a meta port while suffixed packages track any other version or development snapshots. For example,

- security/gnupg vs. security/gnupg1 
- graphics/glfw vs. graphics/glfw2

or

- lang/python vs. lang/pythonXY
- lang/gcc vs. lang/gccXY

Also new FreeBSD users (former Linux or macOS) may expect "ncdu" to track the latest:

Arch, Gentoo, Homebrew, MacPorts, NixPkgs, openmamba, PkgSrc, Solus, T2 SDE use plain "ncdu" for 2.x series according to https://repology.org/project/ncdu/versions (ignore 1.*)

vs.

Alpine, FreeBSD, Void use "ncdu2" for 2.x series according to https://repology.org/project/ncdu2/versions
